name: Animation COMP
description: The Animation Component is a special component used for creating keyframe animation channels. The component contains a pre-defined network utilizing a Keyframe CHOP and a number of Table DATs to define the animated CHOP channels.
summary: The Animation Component is a special component used for creating keyframe animation channels. The component contains a pre-defined network utilizing a Keyframe CHOP and a number of Table DATs to defin

parameters.name: Time Reference
parameters.label: Time Reference
parameters.description: Parameter of this operator. See operator description for details.

parameters.name: Play Mode
parameters.label: Play Mode
parameters.description: Specifies the method used to playback the animation or allows the output the entire animation curve.

parameters.name: Locked to Timeline
parameters.label: Locked to Timeline
parameters.description: locked - This mode locks the animation position to the timeline. Scrubbing or jumping in the timeline will change the animation position accordingly. The parameters Play, Speed, Cue, and Cue  are disabled in this mode since the timeline is directly controlling the animation's position. input - This mode allows the user to specify a particular position in the animation using the index input on the Animation  (the  input to the Animation ).  The Input Index Unit parameter can be used to change the units of the input channel. Use this mode for random access to any location in the animation for maximum flexibility. sequential - This mode continually plays regardless of the timeline position (the Index parameter is disabled). Reset and Speed parameters below are enabled to allow some control. outputrange - This option outputs the entire animation channel range.  This is useful for using the Animation  to create custom lookup curves/channels.

parameters.name: Play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: play - Animation plays when On and stops when Off. This animation playback control is only available when Play Mode is Sequential.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: speed - This is a speed multiplier which only works when Play Mode is Sequential. A value of 1 is the default playback speed. A value of 2 is double speed, 0.5 is half speed and so on. Negative values will play the animation backwards.

parameters.name: Cue
parameters.label: Cue
parameters.description: cue - Jumps to Cue  when set to 1. Only available when Play Mode is Sequential.

parameters.name: Cue Pulse
parameters.label: Cue Pulse
parameters.description: cuepulse - Instantly jumps to the Cue .

parameters.name: Cue Point
parameters.label: Cue Point
parameters.description: cuepoint - Set any index in the animation as a point to jump to. Only available when Play Mode is Sequential.

parameters.name: Cue Point Unit
parameters.label: Cue Point Unit
parameters.description: cuepointunit - Units used when setting the Cue  parameter.

parameters.name: Input Index Unit
parameters.label: Input Index Unit
parameters.description:
inputindexunit -  - When  is set to Use Input Index use this menu to choose the units for the index input channel. For example, choose between setting the index with frames or seconds. The Units X option sets the index to use the key information directly from the key  table inside the Animation , disregarding any custom settings found in the attributes  table.

 samples - frames - seconds - fraction - xunits -

parameters.name: Samples
parameters.label: Samples
parameters.description: samples - frames - seconds - fraction - xunits -

parameters.name: Cyclic Range
parameters.label: Cyclic Range
parameters.description:
cyclic -  - Adapts the range of the animation for cyclic or non-cyclic input indices. When using a cyclic input index the lookup value for index 0.0 and 1.0 result in the same value. To avoid this, set Cyclic Range to Yes and the lookup will cycle smoothly.

 auto - Checks the right extend condition of each channel (Assumes type cycle and mirror are cyclic lookups). yes - Cycles the output when index is outside the animation range. no - Does not cycle the output when index is outside the animation range.

parameters.name: Automatic
parameters.label: Automatic
parameters.description: auto - Checks the right extend condition of each channel (Assumes type cycle and mirror are cyclic lookups). yes - Cycles the output when index is outside the animation range. no - Does not cycle the output when index is outside the animation range.

parameters.name: Specify Edit Attributes
parameters.label: Specify Edit Attributes
parameters.description: specifyedit - Turn this on to enable the edit attributes parameter below.

parameters.name: Edit Origin
parameters.label: Edit Origin
parameters.description: editorigin - Changes the origin of the animation channel edits. This does not change the data stored in the key  table, but it does effect the channels display in the graph and playback of the animation.

parameters.name: Edit Rate
parameters.label: Edit Rate
parameters.description: editrate - Changes the rate of the animation channel edits. This does not change the data stored in the key  table, but it does effect the channels display in the graph and playback of the animation.

parameters.name: Edit Animation...
parameters.label: Edit Animation...
parameters.description: editanimation - Clicking this button will open this Animation  in the Animation Editor.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description:
rangetype -  - Set the working range for the Animation .

 timeline - Uses the range set in the timeline specified by the Time  parameter on the previous Animation parameter page. custom - Set a custom range using the Start and End parameters below.

parameters.name: Timeline
parameters.label: Timeline
parameters.description: timeline - Uses the range set in the timeline specified by the Time  parameter on the previous Animation parameter page. custom - Set a custom range using the Start and End parameters below.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: start - Start of the Custom range, expressed in units seconds, frames or samples.

parameters.name: Start Unit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description: startunit - Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: End
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: end - End of the Custom range, expressed in units seconds, frames or samples.

parameters.name: End Unit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description: endunit - Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: Trim Left
parameters.label: Trim Left
parameters.description:
tleft -  - Determines the output of the channels when past the 'End' position. Does not affect Play Mode = Output Full Range, to manipulate the Extend Conditions of that mode adjust the Extend parameters of the Keyframe CHOP inside the Animation .

 hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope before the start of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.label: Hold
parameters.description: hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope before the start of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Trim Right
parameters.label: Trim Right
parameters.description:
tright -  - Determines the output of the channels when before the 'Start' position. Does not affect Play Mode = Output Full Range, to manipulate the Extend Conditions of that mode adjust the Extend parameters of the Keyframe CHOP inside the Animation .

 hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope after the end of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Trim Default
parameters.label: Trim Default
parameters.description: tdefault - The value used for the Default Value trim conditio above.

parameters.name: Extension
parameters.label: Extension
parameters.description:
ext - Sequence of info for creating extensions on this component



  ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.


    
  ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.



  ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Object
parameters.label: Object
parameters.description: ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.

parameters.name: Promote
parameters.label: Promote
parameters.description: ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.label: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.description: reinitextensions - Recompile all extension objects. Normally extension objects are compiled only when they are referenced and their definitions have changed.

parameters.name: Parent Shortcut
parameters.label: Parent Shortcut
parameters.description: parentshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as the path to that component. See Parent Shortcut.

parameters.name: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.label: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.description: opshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere at all as the path to that component. See Global OP Shortcut.

parameters.name: Internal OP
parameters.label: Internal OP
parameters.description:
iop - Sequence header for internal operators.



  iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.



  iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Shortcut
parameters.label: Shortcut
parameters.description: iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: OP
parameters.label: OP
parameters.description: iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Operator Viewer
parameters.label: Operator Viewer
parameters.description: opviewer - Select which operator's node viewer to use when the Node View parameter above is set to Operator .

parameters.name: Enable Cloning
parameters.label: Enable Cloning
parameters.description: enablecloning - Control if the OP should be actively cloneing. Turning this off causes this node to stop cloning it's ' Master'.

parameters.name: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.label: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.description: enablecloningpulse - Instantaneously clone the contents.

parameters.name: Clone Master
parameters.label: Clone Master
parameters.description: clone -  to a component used as the Master Clone.

parameters.name: Load on Demand
parameters.label: Load on Demand
parameters.description: loadondemand - Loads the component into memory only when required. Good to use for components that are not always used in the project.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox
parameters.label: Enable External .tox
parameters.description: enableexternaltox - When on (default), the external  file will be loaded when the  starts and the contents of the  will match that of the external . This can be turned off to avoid loading from the referenced external  on startup if desired (the contents of the  are instead loaded from the  file). Useful if you wish to have a  reference an external  but not always load from it unless you specifically push the Re-Init Network parameter button.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.label: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.description: enableexternaltoxpulse - This button will re-load from the external  file (if present).

parameters.name: External .tox Path
parameters.label: External .tox Path
parameters.description: externaltox -  to a  file on disk which will source the component's contents upon start of a . This allows for components to contain networks that can be updated independently. If the  file can not be found, whatever the  file was saved with will be loaded.

parameters.name: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.description: reloadcustom - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's Custom Parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.description: reloadbuiltin - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's built-in parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Save Backup of External
parameters.label: Save Backup of External
parameters.description: savebackup - When this checkbox is enabled, a backup copy of the component specified by the External  parameter is saved in the  file.  This backup copy will be used if the External  can not be found. This may happen if the  was renamed, deleted, or the  file is running on another computer that is missing component media.

parameters.name: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.label: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.description: subcompname - When loading from an External  file, this option allows you to reach into the  and pull out a  and make that the top-level , ignoring everything else in the file (except for the contents of that ). For example if a  file named project1 contains project1/geo1, putting geo1 as the Sub- to Load, will result in geo1 being loaded in place of the current . If this parameter is blank, it just loads the  file normally using the top level  in the file.

parameters.name: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.label: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.description:
relpath -  - Set whether the child file paths within this  are relative to the  itself or the , or inherit from parent.

 inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.

parameters.name: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.label: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.description: inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.
